back_translation: He lifted up His face and saw the rich people bringing their offerings to put into the offering-money box.
And He saw a certain poor widow dropping two small coins in there.
Then He said, 'I tell you the truth, this poor widow has dropped in more than everyone.'
Because all of these took the portion left over from what they ate and used and dropped it as offerings, but this woman took all she had for her livelihood out of her poverty.'
While some disciples were talking together about the temple — that it was beautifully adorned with large stones and all the offerings — He said,
'These things that you see now — the day will come when no stone will remain stacked upon another here; every stone will be torn down and destroyed.'
The disciples asked Him, 'Teacher, when will these things happen, and what signs will there be when these things are about to take place?'
He answered, 'Be careful that no one deceives you. For many people will come claiming My name. They will say, "I am that one," and "The time has drawn near." Do not follow them.
When you hear news of wars and rebellions, do not be shocked and alarmed, because these things must happen first, but the final end will not come immediately.'
Then He said to them, 'Nation will rise up to fight nation, kingdom will rise up to fight kingdom.
There will be great earthquakes; there will be famines and epidemics in various places; there will be frightening sights and great signs from the heavens.
But before all these events, they will arrest and persecute you, handing you over in synagogues and in prisons, bringing you before kings and governors because of My name.
This event will turn into an opportunity for you to be witnesses.
Therefore resolve in your hearts not to prepare defense arguments in advance.
For I will give you mouth and wisdom which none of your adversaries will be able to resist or contradict.
Even parents, siblings, relatives, and friends will betray you, and they will kill some of you to death.
And you will be hated by all people because of My name.
But not a single hair of yours will be lost at all.
By your patient endurance, you will gain your lives back.
'When you see the city of Jerusalem being surrounded by armies, then know that the desolation of this city has drawn near.
At that time, those who are in the region of Judea should flee to the mountains; those in the city should leave the city; and those in the suburban villages should not enter the city at all.
Because those days are days of judgment-punishment, so that everything written in the Scriptures may be completely fulfilled.
In those days, very sorrowful for pregnant women and mothers nursing children, because there will be great suffering on the land and divine wrath falling on this people.

They will fall dead by the edge of the sword, and will be taken captive into various countries, and the city of Jerusalem will be trampled by the Gentiles, until the appointed times of the Gentiles are completely fulfilled.
'There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars, and on the earth various nations will be in distress, bewildered by the roaring of the sea and the tumultuous waves.
All people will faint from fear and from anticipation of what is about to happen to the world, because the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
At that very time, they will see 'the Son of Man coming in the clouds, with power and great glory.'
When these events begin to happen, stand up straight and lift up your heads, because your redemption has drawn near.'
He told them a parable, 'Look at the fig tree and every kind of tree.
When those trees begin to sprout leaves, you yourselves can see and know that summer has drawn near.
Likewise, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God has drawn near.
I tell you the truth, the people of this generation will not yet pass away, until all these things happen.
Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will not pass away at all.
'But be careful of yourselves, or else your hearts will become preoccupied with dissipation-carousing, drunkenness, and worries in daily life, and that day will come upon you suddenly.
Like a snare — because that day will come upon all people who dwell on the face of the whole earth.
Always stay alert, praying that you will have enough strength to escape from all these things that are about to happen, and to be able to stand before the Son of Man.'
In the daytime He was teaching in the temple; at night He went out to stay at the mountain called the Mount of Olives.
Then all the people would come to Him in the early morning at the temple to hear Him.
